content.full_text:
Californians for Constitutional Government

P.O. Box 1107
Pleasanton, CA 94566
(510) 426-8756

James Zuchelli
Chair

12-31-91

Fair Political Practises Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Dear Sirs,

We read in the Capitol Weekly about your Advice Letters and wonder if they affect us in any way. If you could tell us if we are affected by one of these Advice Letters and answer a few questions, we would appreciate it.

Stiles, Jr. (I-91-457) referred to a newsletter published close to election time being an "express advocacy". We put out a newsletter sporadically and we will be endorsing some candidates at election time and attacking others. How are we affected by this?

If a current elected member of government sends us a check to help with newsletter costs ($25.00) and we later endorse that person, (because that person endorses our general policies) are we affected or do we have to report that donation?

If a member of our organizational group were to run for elected office and we endorsed that person, are there any rules or regulations that we would have to follow?

We are a small grass-roots organization, poorly funded, and while we plan to endorse some candidates and attack others, our platform is what motivates us, not any particular candidates platform. We do not give money to any candidates.

Cordially,
James Zuchelli,
Chair, CCG
